headline: Broadcom builds custom chip for OpenAI
detail: Broadcom unveiled a custom AI chip designed for OpenAI, directly challenging Nvidia's dominance in AI infrastructure. This is a concrete positive catalyst for AVGO, your largest (and currently underwater) position — it validates Broadcom's AI custom silicon strategy. Watch if the stock responds with enough strength to change your trim calculus.

headline: Warsh may get green light to hike rates
detail: An analyst is suggesting the White House is signaling Kevin Warsh could pursue rate hikes if he becomes Fed chair. Higher rates are a direct valuation headwind for every high-multiple tech and chip name in your book — PLTR, AVGO, CRWD, SMH all get hit when rate expectations rise. This is the macro risk most likely to push your underwater positions deeper into the red.

headline: Qualcomm stock surges on strong numbers
detail: Qualcomm is rallying hard on strong results, which is a positive read-through for the broader semiconductor sector. This is good context for SMH, your one chip name currently in the green, and may offer modest support across your chip exposure. Doesn't directly fix your AVGO or PLTR situations but confirms chips aren't universally broken.

headline: JPMorgan $50B buyback, Goldman raises dividend after stress test
detail: Major banks cleared the Fed stress test with flying colors, signaling the financial system is stable and capital is being returned to shareholders. This reduces systemic risk fears and supports a 'risk-on' backdrop that generally helps tech and growth names. Not a direct mover for your holdings but a modest positive for overall market sentiment.
